_publications._publicationTitle: A platform for rapid patient-derived cutaneous neurofibroma organoid establishment and screening

_contextSnippet: Drug screening of 43 targeted kinase inhibitors at 1 μM identified 4 compounds with average residual cell viability ≤50% across all five specimens: copanlisib, onalespib, linsitinib, and digoxin; sample-to-sample differences in response observed
details: Organoid included in 43-compound kinase inhibitor screen (1 μM, 24h post-treatment ATP assay); 4 drugs consistently reduced viability ≤50% across all tested specimens (NF0002, NF0004×2, NF0009, NF0012): copanlisib (PI3K inhibitor), onalespib (Hsp90 inhibitor), linsitinib (IGF-1R inhibitor), digoxin (Na+/K+-ATPase inhibitor); staurosporine (1 μM) used as positive control; sample-to-sample differences in drug responses noted across patients
